symptoms.symptom_name: Acute proximal (hip/thigh-girdle) leg weakness with inability to weight-bear and a fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Alcohol-induced pseudo-Cushing's syndrome with proximal myopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cortisol excess produces a proximal (girdle) myopathy; combined with direct alcoholic myopathy this explains the weakness and fall.
Chronic alcohol directly injures proximal skeletal muscle.
Inability to weight-bear after a fall mandates in-person fracture exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent rounded, puffy face and central abdominal fullness (Cushingoid habitus, patient/carer observable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rounded plethoric face and central adiposity are core features of hypercortisolism.
The same habitus is the red herring pulling toward a primary endocrine cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Very heavy daily alcohol use (~15 standard drinks/day, long-standing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic heavy alcohol is the causative driver of the reversible hypercortisolism and the discriminator from true Cushing's.
Heavy dependence means any reduction/cessation risks withdrawal seizures and delirium tremens.

symptoms.symptom_name: No spontaneous severe bruising and no thin/fragile skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe dermal atrophy, easy bruising and broad violaceous striae characterise established true Cushing's; their absence favours a pseudo-Cushing state.
Headache and a thumping-in-the-head sensation are consistent with raised blood pressure; needs in-person measurement and a safety-net for hypertensive emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent new coarse facial hair (mild hirsutism)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild androgenic features can accompany hypercortisolism; non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood pressure 155/95 mmHg and resting tachycardia HR 102 bpm (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective mild hypertension and tachycardia — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers a hypertensive tendency from the headache/palpitations, not a measured number.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical proximal lower-limb weakness power 4/5 without focal neurological deficit (on examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Alcohol-induced pseudo-Cushing's syndrome with proximal myopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Moderate (4/5) symmetrical proximal weakness fits a hypercortisolaemic/alcoholic myopathy rather than the profound myopathy of established true Cushing's.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bony tenderness or deformity; painless hip range of motion / able to straight-leg raise (fracture-clearance exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring in-person examination lowers fracture likelihood — but this is a clinician finding the AI cannot obtain, so the AI must still refer for in-person clearance.

symptoms.symptom_name: Early-morning serum cortisol and comprehensive metabolic panel (confirmatory biochemistry)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Early-morning serum cortisol +/- dynamic (dexamethasone-suppression) testing and comprehensive metabolic panel
investigation_details.expected_result: Not yet available — to be drawn the following morning and interpreted only after a period of abstinence; no value may be fabricated.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without biochemistry the pseudo-Cushing vs true Cushing's distinction cannot be resolved.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biochemistry after abstinence distinguishes reversible pseudo-Cushing from true Cushing's; it is the definitive test and is unavailable in real time in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Cushingoid habitus + mild androgenic features + mild hypertension on a background of very heavy alcohol use forms the pseudo-Cushing pattern; the absence of severe dermal catabolic signs (SYM-004) is what tips it away from true Cushing's.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent same-day in-person assessment with blood-pressure measurement and fracture-clearance examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI cannot measure BP or exclude a fracture; a fall with inability to weight-bear plus a hypertensive-symptom picture needs in-person assessment today.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Early-morning serum cortisol + comprehensive metabolic panel (next morning, interpreted after abstinence)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To distinguish reversible pseudo-Cushing from true Cushing's once the acute situation is stabilised; timing/abstinence matters.
Imaging is not a telehealth action and is premature before biochemistry; ordering it now would be over-investigation driven by the true-Cushing's red herring.
